        {
          by_cases h‚āĄ : (ŌÄ : ‚ĄĚ) ‚ąą {x : ‚ĄĚ | x < 0}
          <;> simp_all [h‚āĄ]
          <;> norm_num at *
          <;>
          (try
            {
              have h‚āÖ : (ŌÄ : ‚ĄĚ) > 0 := Real.pi_pos
              linarith
            })
          <;>
          (try aesop)
        })
      <;>
      (try aesop)
    exact h‚āÉ
  exact h‚āÄ

have h‚āĀ : ({x : ‚ĄĚ | x < 0} : Set ‚ĄĚ) =ŠĶź[őľD] (‚ąÖ : Set ‚ĄĚ) := by
    -- Since őľD is a measure that we can assume is zero away from ŌÄ, and {x | x < 0} does not contain ŌÄ,
    -- we can directly conclude that {x | x < 0} is őľD-a.e. equal to ‚ąÖ.
    have h‚āā : ({x : ‚ĄĚ | x < 0} : Set ‚ĄĚ) =ŠĶź[őľD] (‚ąÖ : Set ‚ĄĚ) := by
      -- We use the fact that őľD is zero on {x | x < 0} to conclude the equality a.e.
      apply MeasureTheory.set_ae_of_forall_not_mem
      <;> simp [őľD]
      <;> (try norm_num)
      <;> (try linarith [Real.pi_pos])
      <;> (try aesop)
    exact h‚āā
  exact h‚āĀ

have hŌÄ : Real.pi > 0 := by
    exact Real.pi_pos
  
  have h1 : (Real.pi : ‚ĄĚ) ‚ąČ {x : ‚ĄĚ | x < 0} := by
    intro h
    have h‚āā : (Real.pi : ‚ĄĚ) < 0 := by simpa using h
    linarith [Real.pi_pos]
  
  have h2 : őľD {x : ‚ĄĚ | x < 0} = 0 := by
    simp [őľD, h1]
    <;>
    aesop
  
  have h3 : {x : ‚ĄĚ | x < 0} =ŠĶź[őľD] (‚ąÖ : Set ‚ĄĚ) := by
    have h4 : őľD {x : ‚ĄĚ | x < 0} = 0 := h2
    have h5 : őľD (‚ąÖ : Set ‚ĄĚ) = 0 := by simp [őľD]
    -- Use the fact that the measure of both sets is zero to conclude the equivalence
    have h6 : {x : ‚ĄĚ | x < 0} =ŠĶź[őľD] (‚ąÖ : Set ‚ĄĚ) := by
      -- Use the fact that the measure of both sets is zero to conclude the equivalence
      have h7 : {x : ‚ĄĚ | x < 0} =ŠĶź[őľD] (‚ąÖ : Set ‚ĄĚ) := by
        -- Use the fact that the measure of both sets is zero to conclude the equivalence
        exact by
          filter_upwards [self_mem_ae_of_measure_zero (by simp [h4] : őľD {x : ‚ĄĚ | x < 0} = 0)] with x hx
          simp_all [Set.mem_empty_iff_false]
          <;>
          aesop
      exact h7
    exact h6
  
  exact h3

have h : {x : ‚ĄĚ | x < 0} =ŠĶź[őľD] (‚ąÖ : Set ‚ĄĚ) := by
    have h‚āĀ : őľD {x : ‚ĄĚ | x < 0} = 0 := by
      simp [őľD, Set.mem_setOf_eq]
      <;>
      norm_num
      <;>
      (try { contradiction }) <;>
      (try { aesop })
    have h‚āā : őľD (‚ąÖ : Set ‚ĄĚ) = 0 := by
      simp [őľD]
    have h‚āÉ : őľD {x : ‚ĄĚ | x < 0} = őľD (‚ąÖ : Set ‚ĄĚ) := by
      rw [h‚āĀ, h‚āā]
    -- Use the fact that the measures are equal to conclude that the sets are almost everywhere equal.
    have h‚āĄ : {x : ‚ĄĚ | x < 0} =ŠĶź[őľD] (‚ąÖ : Set ‚ĄĚ) := by
      rw [MeasureTheory.ae_of_forall_sets_of_nonneg] <;>
        (try { simp_all [őľD, Set.mem_setOf_eq] <;> norm_num <;> aesop }) <;>
        (try { intro s hs <;> simp_all [őľD, Set.mem_setOf_eq] <;> norm_num <;> aesop }) <;>
        (try { intro s hs <;> simp_all [őľD, Set.mem_setOf_eq] <;> norm_num <;> aesop })
    exact h‚āĄ
  exact h

-- The intersection of two neighborhoods is a neighborhood
    have h‚āĀ : {x : őĪ | p' x} ‚ąą ūĚďĚ x := hT'
    have h‚āā : {x : őĪ | q' x} ‚ąą ūĚďĚ x := hS'
    exact Filter.inter_mem h‚āĀ h‚āā
  have h‚āÉ : ( ({x : őĪ | p x} ‚ą© {x : őĪ | q x}) ‚ą™ ({x : őĪ | p' x} ‚ą© {x : őĪ | q' x}) ) ‚ąą ūĚďĚ x := by
    -- The union of two neighborhoods is a neighborhood
    have h‚āĀ : {x : őĪ | p x} ‚ą© {x : őĪ | q x} ‚ąą ūĚďĚ x := h‚āĀ
    have h‚āā : {x : őĪ | p' x} ‚ą© {x : őĪ | q' x} ‚ąą ūĚďĚ x := h‚āā
    exact Filter.union_mem h‚āĀ h‚āā
  -- The set {x | p x ‚ąß q x ‚ą® p' x ‚ąß q' x} is the union of the two intersections
  have h‚āĄ : ( ({x : őĪ | p x} ‚ą© {x : őĪ | q x}) ‚ą™ ({x : őĪ | p' x} ‚ą© {x : őĪ | q' x}) ) = {x : őĪ | (p x ‚ąß q x) ‚ą® (p' x ‚ąß q' x)} := by
    ext x
    simp only [Set.mem_union, Set.mem_setOf_eq, Set.mem_inter_iff]
    <;>
    tauto
  rw [h‚āĄ] at h‚āÉ
  exact h‚āÉ

have h‚āĀ‚āĀ : Real.pi ‚ąą (‚čÉ n : ‚Ąē, s n) := by

have h : {x : ‚ĄĚ | x < 0} =ŠĶź[őľD] (‚ąÖ : Set ‚ĄĚ) := by

    have h‚āā : őľD (‚ąÖ : Set ‚ĄĚ) = 0 := by

    -- Use the fact that the measure of {x : ‚ĄĚ | x < 0} is 0 to conclude the almost everywhere equality.
    have h‚āÉ : {x : ‚ĄĚ | x < 0} =ŠĶź[őľD] (‚ąÖ : Set ‚ĄĚ) := by

have h : {x : ‚ĄĚ | x < 0} ‚ąą ūĚĎöD ‚ą® {x : ‚ĄĚ | x < 0} ‚ąą ūĚĎöDŠ∂ú := by

have h‚āÄ : őľD {x : ‚ĄĚ | x < 0} = 0 := by
    simp [őľD]
    <;>
    norm_num [Real.pi_pos.le] <;>
    (try { linarith [Real.pi_gt_three] }) <;>

        obtain ‚ü®n, hn‚ü© := h

have h : {x : ‚ĄĚ | x < 0} =ŠĶź[őľD] (‚ąÖ : Set ‚ĄĚ) := by
    -- Prove that the set {x : ‚ĄĚ | x < 0} has measure zero under őľD
    have h‚āĀ : őľD {x : ‚ĄĚ | x < 0} = 0 := by
      -- Check if ŌÄ is in the set {x : ‚ĄĚ | x < 0}
      have h‚āā : Real.pi ‚ąČ {x : ‚ĄĚ | x < 0} := by
        -- Since ŌÄ ‚Čą 3.14 > 0, ŌÄ is not in the set
        norm_num [Real.pi_pos]
        <;>
        linarith [Real.pi_pos]
      -- Therefore, the measure of {x : ‚ĄĚ | x < 0} is 0
      simp [őľD, h‚āā]
    -- Use the fact that the measure of {x : ‚ĄĚ | x < 0} is 0 to conclude the proof
    have h‚āā : {x : ‚ĄĚ | x < 0} =ŠĶź[őľD] (‚ąÖ : Set ‚ĄĚ) := by
      -- Use the outer measure property to show that the set is almost everywhere equal to the empty set
      have h‚āÉ : őľD {x : ‚ĄĚ | x < 0} = 0 := h‚āĀ
      have h‚āĄ : őľD (‚ąÖ : Set ‚ĄĚ) = 0 := by simp [őľD]
      -- Use the fact that the measures are equal to conclude the proof
      have h‚āÖ : {x : ‚ĄĚ | x < 0} =ŠĶź[őľD] (‚ąÖ : Set ‚ĄĚ) := by
        -- Use the fact that the measures are equal to conclude the proof
        exact MeasureTheory.ae_of_measure_eq_zero (by
          -- Prove that the measure of the set is zero
          simp_all [h‚āÉ])
      -- Conclude the proof
      exact h‚āÖ
    -- Conclude the proof
    exact h‚āā
  -- Conclude the proof
  exact h
